revision.text:
和田乃神社とは[[東京都]][[青梅市]]日向和田2-317に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 祭神は[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]、[[イワナガヒメ|磐長比売神]]、[[カヤノヒメ|茅野比売神]]の三神を祭る。 旧和田村の総鎮守であった。
最寄り駅は[[青梅線]][[宮ノ平駅]]で、駅名はこの神社に由来する。
例祭は9月の第一日曜日で、[[小学生]]による奉納[[相撲]]が行われている。

'''和田乃神社'''<!--（よみがな）-->とは[[東京都]][[青梅市]]日向和田2-317に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
祭神は[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]、[[イワナガヒメ|磐長比売神]]、[[カヤノヒメ|茅野比売神]]の三神を祭る。 旧和田村の総鎮守であった。
最寄り駅は[[JR]][[青梅線]][[宮ノ平駅]]で、駅名はこの神社に由来する。
例祭は[[9月]]の第一[[日曜日]]で、[[小学生]]による奉納[[相撲]]が行われている。
[[Category:東京都の神社|?]]

'''和田乃神社'''(わだのじんじゃ)とは[[東京都]][[青梅市]]日向和田2-317に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
祭神は[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]、[[イワナガヒメ|磐長比売神]]、[[カヤノヒメ|茅野比売神]]の三神を祭る。 旧和田村の総鎮守であった。
最寄り駅は[[JR]][[青梅線]][[宮ノ平駅]]で、駅名はこの神社に由来する。
例祭は[[9月]]の第一[[日曜日]]で、[[小学生]]による奉納[[相撲]]が行われている。
[[Category:東京都の神社|わ]]

revision.comment: 概要、周辺施設など。
revision.text:
'''和田乃神社'''（わだのじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[青梅市]][[日向和田]]（ひなたわだ）2-317<ref>[[西多摩郡]][[日向和田村]]、神奈川県[[青梅町]]日向和田</ref>に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 概要 == 祭神は[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]、[[イワナガヒメ|磐長比売神]]、[[カヤノヒメ|茅野比売神]]の三神を祭る。 旧[[日向和田村|和田村]]の総鎮守であった。 最寄り駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[青梅線]][[宮ノ平駅]]で、駅名はこの神社に由来する。 例祭は9月の第一日曜日で、[[小学生]]による奉納[[相撲]]が行われている。
== 周辺施設など == * [[東京消防庁]][[青梅消防署]]日向和田出張所 * 臨川庭園 * [[明白院]]：青梅[[七福神]]の一つ。
{{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わたのしんしや}} [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:青梅市の歴史]] [[Category:青梅市の建築物]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=東京都}}

'''和田乃神社'''（わだのじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[青梅市]][[日向和田]]（ひなたわだ）2丁目317<ref>数次の町村合併を経て現表記に至る。従前は[[神奈川県]][[西多摩郡]][[日向和田村]]、神奈川県西多摩郡[[青梅町]]日向和田、[[東京府]]西多摩郡青梅町日向和田</ref>に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 概要 == 祭神は[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]、[[イワナガヒメ|磐長比売神]]、[[カヤノヒメ|茅野比売神]]の三神を祭る。 旧[[日向和田村|和田村]]の総鎮守であった。 最寄り駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[青梅線]][[宮ノ平駅]]で、駅名はこの神社に由来する。 例祭は9月の第一日曜日で、[[小学生]]による奉納[[相撲]]が行われている。
== 周辺施設など == * [[東京消防庁]][[青梅消防署]]日向和田出張所 * 臨川庭園 * [[明白院]]：多摩青梅[[七福神]]の一つで[[福禄寿]]を祀る<ref>[http://ome7.tokyo/meibakuin/ 明白院 青梅七福神めぐり]. 青梅七福神会</ref> * [[日向和田保育園]]

revision.comment: 画像追加
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 和田乃神社 |画像=[[File:Wadano Jinja.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[東京都]][[青梅市]][[日向和田]]2-317 |位置 = {{Coord|35|47|08.6|N|139|14|09.4|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]、[[イワナガヒメ|磐長比売神]]、[[カヤノヒメ|茅野比売神]] |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=和田乃神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''和田乃神社'''（わだのじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[青梅市]][[日向和田]]（ひなたわだ）2丁目317<ref>数次の町村合併を経て現表記に至る。従前は[[神奈川県]][[西多摩郡]][[日向和田村]]、神奈川県西多摩郡[[青梅町]]日向和田、[[東京府]]西多摩郡青梅町日向和田</ref>に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 概要 == 祭神は[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]、[[イワナガヒメ|磐長比売神]]、[[カヤノヒメ|茅野比売神]]の三神を祭る。 旧[[日向和田村|和田村]]の総鎮守であった。 最寄り駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[青梅線]][[宮ノ平駅]]で、駅名はこの神社に由来する。 例祭は9月の第一日曜日で、[[小学生]]による奉納[[相撲]]が行われている。
== 周辺施設など == * [[東京消防庁]][[青梅消防署]]日向和田出張所 * 臨川庭園 * [[明白院]]：多摩青梅[[七福神]]の一つで[[福禄寿]]を祀る<ref>[http://ome7.tokyo/meibakuin/ 明白院 青梅七福神めぐり]. 青梅七福神会</ref> * [[日向和田保育園]]
== 脚注 == <references/>
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=東京都}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:わたのしんしや}} [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:青梅市の歴史]] [[Category:青梅市の建築物]]

'''Wadano Shrine''' (和田乃神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located at 2-317, {{ill|Hinatawada|ja|日向和田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada|qq=|1=Hinatawada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276771}}, [[:en:Ōme, Tokyo|Ōme, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]<ref>After several municipal mergers, the current notation was adopted. Previously, it was located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishitama District, Tokyo|Nishi-Tama District]] {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kanagawa Prefecture, Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|qq=|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada, and in [[:en:Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)|Tokyo Metropolis]] Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|qq=|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada</ref>.

== Overview ==
The enshrined deities are the three gods: [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Great Mountain Deity]], [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Rock Long Princess]], and [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Reed Princess]].
It was the head shrine of the former {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|lt=Wada Village|lt_ja=和田村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The nearest station is [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Ōme Line|Ome Line]][[:en:Miyanohira Station|Miyanodaira Station]], whose name is derived from this shrine.
The annual festival is held on the first Sunday of September, featuring sumo wrestling dedicated by {{ill|Elementary School Students|ja|小学生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elementary School Students|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 在籍者 (学習者)|1=registered student|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104044411}} and [[:en:Sumo|Sumo Wrestling]].

== Surrounding Facilities, etc. ==
* [[:en:Tokyo Fire Department|Tokyo Fire Department]]{{ill|Ome Fire Station|ja|青梅消防署|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Fire Station|qq=|1=Ome Fire Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662007}} Hinatawada Branch Office
* Rinchuan Garden
* {{ill|Meibakuin|ja|明白院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meibakuin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: One of the Tama-Ome [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], enshrining [[:en:Fukurokuju|Fukurokuju]]<ref>[http://ome7.tokyo/meibakuin/ Meibakuin Tama-Ome Seven Lucky Gods Tour]. Tama-Ome Seven Lucky Gods Association</ref>
* {{ill|Hinatawada Nursery School|ja|日向和田保育園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Nursery School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Wadano Shrine''' (和田乃神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located at 2-317, {{ill|Hinatawada|ja|日向和田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada|1=Hinatawada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276771}}, [[:en:Ōme, Tokyo|Ōme, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]<ref>After several municipal mergers, the current notation was adopted. Previously, it was located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishitama District, Tokyo|Nishi-Tama District]] {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kanagawa Prefecture, Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada, and in [[:en:Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)|Tokyo Metropolis]] Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada</ref>.

== Overview ==
The enshrined deities are the three gods: [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Great Mountain Deity]], [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Rock Long Princess]], and [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Reed Princess]].
It was the head shrine of the former {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|lt=Wada Village|lt_ja=和田村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The nearest station is [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Ōme Line|Ome Line]][[:en:Miyanohira Station|Miyanodaira Station]], whose name is derived from this shrine.
The annual festival is held on the first Sunday of September, featuring sumo wrestling dedicated by {{ill|Elementary School Students|ja|小学生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elementary School Students|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 在籍者 (学習者)|1=registered student|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104044411}} and [[:en:Sumo|Sumo Wrestling]].

'''Wadano Shrine''' (和田乃神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located at 2-317, {{ill|Hinatawada|ja|日向和田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada|1=Hinatawada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276771}}, [[:en:Ōme, Tokyo|Ōme, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]<ref>After several municipal mergers, the current notation was adopted. Previously, it was located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishitama District, Tokyo|Nishi-Tama District]] {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kanagawa Prefecture, Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada, and in [[:en:Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)|Tokyo Metropolis]] Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada</ref>.

== Overview ==
The enshrined deities are the three gods: [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Great Mountain Deity]], [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Rock Long Princess]], and [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Reed Princess]].
It was the head shrine of the former {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|lt=Wada Village|lt_ja=和田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The nearest station is [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Ōme Line|Ome Line]][[:en:Miyanohira Station|Miyanodaira Station]], whose name is derived from this shrine.
The annual festival is held on the first Sunday of September, featuring sumo wrestling dedicated by {{ill|Elementary School Students|ja|小学生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elementary School Students|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 在籍者 (学習者)|1=registered student|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104044411}} and [[:en:Sumo|Sumo Wrestling]].

== Surrounding Facilities, etc. ==
* [[:en:Tokyo Fire Department|Tokyo Fire Department]]{{ill|Ome Fire Station|ja|青梅消防署|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Fire Station|1=Ome Fire Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662007}} Hinatawada Branch Office
* Rinchuan Garden
* {{ill|Meibakuin|ja|明白院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meibakuin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: One of the Tama-Ome [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], enshrining [[:en:Fukurokuju|Fukurokuju]]<ref>[http://ome7.tokyo/meibakuin/ Meibakuin Tama-Ome Seven Lucky Gods Tour]. Tama-Ome Seven Lucky Gods Association</ref>
* {{ill|Hinatawada Nursery School|ja|日向和田保育園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Nursery School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

The annual festival is held on the first Sunday of September, featuring sumo wrestling dedicated by {{ill|Elementary School Students|ja|小学生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elementary School Students|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 在籍者 (学習者)|1=registered student|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104044411}} and [[:en:Sumo|Sumo Wrestling]].

== Surrounding Facilities, etc. ==

Previously, it was located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishitama District, Tokyo|Nishi-Tama District]] {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kanagawa Prefecture, Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada, and in [[:en:Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)|Tokyo Metropolis]] Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada</ref>.

'''Wadano Shrine''' (和田乃神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located at 2-317, {{ill|Hinatawada|ja|日向和田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada|1=Hinatawada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276771}}, [[:en:Ōme, Tokyo|Ōme, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]<ref>After several municipal mergers, the current notation was adopted. Previously, it was located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishitama District, Tokyo|Nishi-Tama District]] {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kanagawa Prefecture, Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada, and in [[:en:Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)|Tokyo Metropolis]] Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada</ref>.

== Overview ==
The enshrined deities are the three gods: [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Great Mountain Deity]], [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Rock Long Princess]], and [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Reed Princess]].
It was the head shrine of the former {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|lt=Wada Village|lt_ja=和田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The nearest station is [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Ōme Line|Ome Line]][[:en:Miyanohira Station|Miyanodaira Station]], whose name is derived from this shrine.
The annual festival is held on the first Sunday of September, featuring sumo wrestling dedicated by {{ill|Elementary School Students|ja|小学生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elementary School Students|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 在籍者 (学習者)|1=registered student|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104044411}} and [[:en:Sumo|Sumo Wrestling]].

== Surrounding Facilities, etc. ==
* [[:en:Tokyo Fire Department|Tokyo Fire Department]]{{ill|Ome Fire Station|ja|青梅消防署|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Fire Station|1=Ome Fire Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662007}} Hinatawada Branch Office
* Rinchuan Garden
* {{ill|Meibakuin|ja|明白院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meibakuin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: One of the Tama-Ome [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], enshrining [[:en:Fukurokuju|Fukurokuju]]<ref>[http://ome7.tokyo/meibakuin/ Meibakuin Tama-Ome Seven Lucky Gods Tour]. Tama-Ome Seven Lucky Gods Association</ref>
* {{ill|Hinatawada Nursery School|ja|日向和田保育園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Nursery School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和田乃神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和田乃神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和田乃神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11417905]] Wikidata name: Wada Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Wadano Shrine |image = File:Wadano Jinja.JPG |Location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}{{ill|Ōme, Tokyo|en|Ōme, Tokyo|WD=Q237683|lt=Ōme, Tokyo}}{{ill|Hinatawada|ja|日向和田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada|1=Hinatawada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276771}}2-317 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|47|08.6|N|139|14|09.4|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Great Mountain Deity}}、{{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Rock Long Princess}}、{{ill|Kaya-no-hime|en|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878|lt=Reed Princess}} |Rank = |Founded = Unknown |MainHall = |AlternateName = |EmaAndOther = |AnnualFestival = |Ceremonies = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Wadano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Ome|WD=Q237683}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.78572222222222|N|139.23594444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wadano Shrine''' (和田乃神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located at 2-317, {{ill|Hinatawada|ja|日向和田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada|1=Hinatawada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276771}}, {{ill|Ōme, Tokyo|en|Ōme, Tokyo|WD=Q237683|lt=Ōme, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}<ref>After several municipal mergers, the current notation was adopted. Previously, it was located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Nishitama District, Tokyo|en|Nishitama District, Tokyo|WD=Q1048689|lt=Nishi-Tama District}} {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kanagawa Prefecture, Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada, and in {{ill|Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)|en|Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)|WD=Q1189121|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}} Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada</ref>.
== Overview == The enshrined deities are the three gods: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Great Mountain Deity}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Rock Long Princess}}, and {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|en|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878|lt=Reed Princess}}. It was the head shrine of the former {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|lt=Wada Village|lt_ja=和田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The nearest station is {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR}}{{ill|Ōme Line|en|Ōme Line|WD=Q385398|lt=Ome Line}}{{ill|Miyanohira Station|en|Miyanohira Station|WD=Q8013443|lt=Miyanodaira Station}}, whose name is derived from this shrine. The annual festival is held on the first Sunday of September, featuring sumo wrestling dedicated by {{ill|Elementary School Students|ja|小学生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elementary School Students|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 在籍者 (学習者)|1=registered student|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104044411}} and {{ill|Sumo|en|Sumo|WD=Q40561|lt=Sumo Wrestling}}.
== Surrounding Facilities, etc. == * {{ill|Tokyo Fire Department|en|Tokyo Fire Department|WD=Q1162928|lt=Tokyo Fire Department}}{{ill|Ome Fire Station|ja|青梅消防署|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Fire Station|1=Ome Fire Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662007}} Hinatawada Branch Office * Rinchuan Garden * {{ill|Meibakuin|ja|明白院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meibakuin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: One of the Tama-Ome {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}, enshrining {{ill|Fukurokuju|en|Fukurokuju|WD=Q780797|lt=Fukurokuju}}<ref>[http://ome7.tokyo/meibakuin/ Meibakuin Tama-Ome Seven Lucky Gods Tour]. Tama-Ome Seven Lucky Gods Association</ref> * {{ill|Hinatawada Nursery School|ja|日向和田保育園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Nursery School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Footnotes == <references/>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Wadano Shrine |image = File:Wadano Jinja.JPG |Location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|Ōme, Tokyo|en|Ōme, Tokyo|WD=Q237683|lt=Ōme, Tokyo|1=Ōme, Tokyo}}{{ill|Hinatawada|ja|日向和田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada|1=Hinatawada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276771}}2-317 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|47|08.6|N|139|14|09.4|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Great Mountain Deity|1=Ōyamatsumi}}、{{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Rock Long Princess|1=Iwanaga-hime}}、{{ill|Kaya-no-hime|en|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878|lt=Reed Princess|1=Kaya-no-hime}} |Rank = |Founded = Unknown |MainHall = |AlternateName = |EmaAndOther = |AnnualFestival = |Ceremonies = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Wadano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Ome|WD=Q237683|1=Ōme, Tokyo}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.78572222222222|N|139.23594444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wadano Shrine''' (和田乃神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at 2-317, {{ill|Hinatawada|ja|日向和田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada|1=Hinatawada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276771}}, {{ill|Ōme, Tokyo|en|Ōme, Tokyo|WD=Q237683|lt=Ōme, Tokyo|1=Ōme, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}<ref>After several municipal mergers, the current notation was adopted. Previously, it was located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Nishitama District, Tokyo|en|Nishitama District, Tokyo|WD=Q1048689|lt=Nishi-Tama District|1=Nishitama District}} {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kanagawa Prefecture, Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada, and in {{ill|Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)|en|Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)|WD=Q1189121|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)}} Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada</ref>.
== Overview == The enshrined deities are the three gods: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Great Mountain Deity|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Rock Long Princess|1=Iwanaga-hime}}, and {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|en|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878|lt=Reed Princess|1=Kaya-no-hime}}. It was the head shrine of the former {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|lt=Wada Village|lt_ja=和田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The nearest station is {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}}{{ill|Ōme Line|en|Ōme Line|WD=Q385398|lt=Ome Line|1=Ōme Line}}{{ill|Miyanohira Station|en|Miyanohira Station|WD=Q8013443|lt=Miyanodaira Station|1=Miyanohira Station}}, whose name is derived from this shrine. The annual festival is held on the first Sunday of September, featuring sumo wrestling dedicated by {{ill|Elementary School Students|ja|小学生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elementary School Students|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 在籍者 (学習者)|1=registered student|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104044411}} and {{ill|Sumo|en|Sumo|WD=Q40561|lt=Sumo Wrestling|1=Sumo}}.
== Surrounding Facilities, etc. == * {{ill|Tokyo Fire Department|en|Tokyo Fire Department|WD=Q1162928|lt=Tokyo Fire Department|1=Tokyo Fire Department}}{{ill|Ome Fire Station|ja|青梅消防署|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Fire Station|1=Q11662007|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662007}} Hinatawada Branch Office * Rinchuan Garden * {{ill|Meibakuin|ja|明白院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meibakuin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: One of the Tama-Ome {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}, enshrining {{ill|Fukurokuju|en|Fukurokuju|WD=Q780797|lt=Fukurokuju|1=Fukurokuju}}<ref>[http://ome7.tokyo/meibakuin/ Meibakuin Tama-Ome Seven Lucky Gods Tour]. Tama-Ome Seven Lucky Gods Association</ref> * {{ill|Hinatawada Nursery School|ja|日向和田保育園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Nursery School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Footnotes == <references/>

'''Wadano Shrine''' (和田乃神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at 2-317, {{ill|Hinatawada|ja|日向和田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada|1=Hinatawada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276771}}, {{ill|Ōme, Tokyo|en|Ōme, Tokyo|WD=Q237683|lt=Ōme, Tokyo|1=Ōme, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}<ref>After several municipal mergers, the current notation was adopted. Previously, it was located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Nishitama District, Tokyo|en|Nishitama District, Tokyo|WD=Q1048689|lt=Nishi-Tama District|1=Nishitama District}} {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in Kanagawa Prefecture, Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada, and in {{ill|Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)|en|Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)|WD=Q1189121|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)}} Nishi-Tama District, {{ill|Ome Town|ja|青梅町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Town|1=Ōme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662006}} Hinatawada</ref>.
== Overview == The enshrined deities are the three gods: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Great Mountain Deity|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Rock Long Princess|1=Iwanaga-hime}}, and {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|en|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878|lt=Reed Princess|1=Kaya-no-hime}}. It was the head shrine of the former {{ill|Hinatawada Village|ja|日向和田村|lt=Wada Village|lt_ja=和田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatawada Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The nearest station is {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}}{{ill|Ōme Line|en|Ōme Line|WD=Q385398|lt=Ome Line|1=Ōme Line}}{{ill|Miyanohira Station|en|Miyanohira Station|WD=Q8013443|lt=Miyanodaira Station|1=Miyanohira Station}}, whose name is derived from this shrine. The annual festival is held on the first Sunday of September, featuring sumo wrestling dedicated by {{ill|Elementary School Students|ja|小学生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elementary School Students|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 在籍者 (学習者)|1=registered student|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104044411}} and {{ill|Sumo|en|Sumo|WD=Q40561|lt=Sumo Wrestling|1=Sumo}}.
== Surrounding Facilities, etc. == * {{ill|Tokyo Fire Department|en|Tokyo Fire Department|WD=Q1162928|lt=Tokyo Fire Department|1=Tokyo Fire Department}}{{ill|Ome Fire Station|ja|青梅消防署|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ome Fire Station|1=Q11662007|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662007}} Hinatawada Branch Office * Rinchuan Garden * {{ill|Meibakuin|ja|明白院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meibakuin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: One of the Tama-Ome {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}, enshrining {{ill|Fukurokuju|en|Fukurokuju|WD=Q780797|lt=Fukurokuju|1=Fukurokuju}}<ref>[http://ome7.tokyo/meibakuin/ Meibakuin Tama-Ome Seven Lucky Gods Tour].
